title: Indulgent Iced Argentinian Peanuts Pilaf
description: A monsoon-inspired Argentinian dish featuring peanuts, lamination puff, and rich umami flavors, perfect for a Mediterranean-friendly vessel like a dolsot stone bowl.
instructions: Thaw the lamination puff pastry according to the package instructions.
Cook the Argentinian chorizo in a pan over medium heat until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the thinly sliced red onions and cook until they start to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Roll out the thawed puff pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Place the cooked chorizo, onions, and garlic mixture in the center of the pastry.
Sprinkle the shredded Parmesan cheese over the filling.
Brush the edges of the pastry with olive oil and fold the pastry over the filling to form a triangle or rectangle shape.
Place the pastry in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the pastry is baking, cook the peanuts in a pan over medium heat until fragrant, about 5 minutes.
When the pastry is done, remove it from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Place the cooked peanuts in a dolsot stone bowl and top with the warm pastry.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
